name: Slack
description: Slack message events as redact-and-pass governed evidence (read/ingest).

steps: In your Slack app's Basic Information, copy the Signing Secret.
Bicameral verifies X-Slack-Signature (v0= HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{ts}:{body}') with a 5-minute replay window, constant-time, fail-closed.

webhook.signature_scheme: v0= hex HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{X-Slack-Request-Timestamp}:{raw_body}', 5-minute replay window

steps: Create a Slack app, enable Event Subscriptions, subscribe to message events.
Set the Request URL to your Bicameral webhook receiver (see receiver below); the url_verification handshake is handled.

data.pii_posture: Message text is PII-dense human communication -> passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). author is the OPAQUE Slack user id (e.g. U0123ABC) -- pseudonymous (the operator holds the id->identity mapping; SG-2026-06-05-D), not a name/email. FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop.
data.redaction: redact-and-pass (message text); opaque Slack user id surfaced (pseudonymous)

instructions.text: Create a Slack app with Event Subscriptions on message events, pointing the Request URL at your Bicameral receiver.
Paste the Slack app Signing Secret.
Test the connection: a signed message event should produce one emission (text redact-and-passed); the url_verification handshake + replays are dropped.

wire_gates: Webhook path built + verified: X-Slack-Signature v0= HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{ts}:{body}' + 5-minute replay window (constant-time, fail-closed); url_verification handshake + unverified deliveries normalize to []. Message text redact-and-passed; opaque user id (pseudonymous) surfaced.
Trust tier T2 (read/ingest). Notify/write (T3+) is deferred (ADR-0008, evidence-before-action).
Webhook RECEIPT is operator-runtime (receiver URL + slack_webhook injection). Live flip gated on operator review + a signed live event (ADR-0012).
live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse + X-Slack-Signature verify (v0, 5-min replay) + handshake/dedup are built and harness-proven; message text redact-and-passed, opaque user id surfaced. Gated on operator review + a live signed event with a real signing secret + a provisioned receiver. To flip: create the app + signing secret + Request URL receiver; wire GatewaySink; send a signed test event; review before promoting.
